symptom: Win32 app (EXE format like SSMS) deployment fails or doesn't install on target Windows device
rootCause: Incorrect install/uninstall commands or detection rules for EXE-based Win32 apps packaged via Intune
solution: Use silent install flags (e.g. SSMS-Setup-ENU.exe /install /quiet /norestart), configure file-based detection rule pointing to installed file path (e.g. C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Microsoft SQL Server Tools 17)

symptom: Windows AutoPilot deployment fails with errors like 801C0003 (Something went wrong - AAD join) or 80180018 (Something went wrong - MDM enrollment)
rootCause: Azure AD join failure or MDM enrollment issue during AutoPilot OOBE process
solution: Capture ETW trace: press Shift-F10 at OOBE, run wpr.exe -start AutoPilot.wprp, reproduce issue, run wpr.exe -stop C:\AutoPilot.etl. Analyze in WPA: expand System Activity > Generic Events, check Microsoft.Windows.Shell.CloudExperienceHost.Common (ZTD events) and Microsoft.Windows.Shell.CloudDomainJoin.Client providers. Search for CDJUIError event to find error codes.

symptom: Intune remote commands and policy updates not reaching Windows device immediately, only applying after 8-hour default check-in interval
rootCause: Windows Push Notification Service (WNS) not functioning - Push registry key missing/expired under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments\*\Push, WNS service stopped, or dependency services (ncbservice, Microsoft Account Sign-in Assistant) disabled. Event ID 1116 'Device Compact Ticket request failed with error 0x80070426' indicates Sign-in Assistant issue.
solution: 1) Check HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments\*\Push registry key exists and expiry time is future. 2) Verify WNS service (Windows Push Notification System service) is running. 3) Ensure dependency services started: Network Connection Broker (ncbservice) and Microsoft Account Sign-in Assistant. 4) Check TCP connection to WNS endpoints via 'netstat | findstr ESTABLISHED'. 5) Verify device ticket at HKU\.DEFAULT\Software\Microsoft\IdentityCRL\Immersive\production\Token.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Windows/Intune Remote command Push and Policy assignment u (2).md

symptom: Updated Intune policy shows as Succeeded but removed sub-settings are not actually removed from device, taking up to 7 hours to apply (TattooRemoval delay)
rootCause: TattooRemoval hold-off period (15 hours for CTIP, 7 hours for other tenants) applies when at least one Effective Group has no policy assignments, typically when admin uses only one assignment type (all User or all Device targeting) without mixed deployment
solution: Create a dummy/fake policy targeting impacted devices using the opposite assignment type: if admin has all Device-type assignments, add a User assignment; if all User-type assignments, add a Device assignment. This bypasses the hold-off period. Kusto: DeviceManagementProvider | where ActivityId contains 'Intune_device_ID' | where message contains 'removal'
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Windows/Intune Remote command Push and Policy assignment u (2).md

symptom: After deploying app as Available type to user group, end user unable to see it or it takes very long to appear in Company Portal
rootCause: Effective group membership change causes app catalog query (HttpSubsystem) to return resultCount=0, correlated with EffectiveGroupChangeTime. Even apps assigned to All Users may temporarily disappear during group recalculation.
solution: 1) Check HttpSubsystem Kusto: filter by deviceId and check resultCount for /StatelessIWService/Applications queries. 2) Check PolicyAssignmentProvider for policy assignment count changes. 3) Verify EffectiveGroupMembershipsUserService_RawData for EffectiveGroupChangeTime and IsEffectiveGroupChangeNotifyPending. 4) Compare Company Portal log timestamps with Kusto results.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Troubleshooting Available app deploy to user group (2).md

symptom: iOS VPN profile deploys successfully but Global Protect app unable to pick up the VPN configuration in China
rootCause: China version of Global Protect app uses different bundle ID: com.paloaltonetworks.globalprotect.vpncn (China App Store id=1507179755) vs global version com.paloaltonetworks.globalprotect.vpn (US Store id=1400555706)
solution: Use Custom VPN profile or XML-based profile with correct China bundle ID (com.paloaltonetworks.globalprotect.vpncn). To verify bundle ID: query https://itunes.apple.com/lookup?id={appId}&country={countryCode} and check bundleId field in response.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/iOS/VPN profile deploy succeed but Global Protect app.md
